loops.description: Conflict loop: new findings trigger re-entry into observation and reassessment
Tight reassessment loop: conflicting findings redirect decision without full regeneration

branches.condition: New findings conflict with current view
Conflict is minor; working hypothesis survives
Conflict invalidates hypothesis; requires new candidates

candidate_missing_primitives.label: Rank
candidate_missing_primitives.rationale: Prioritize covers ordering but the step of assigning relative plausibility weights to candidates feels distinct from selection; a dedicated Rank primitive might better capture likelihood ordering before Decide

candidate_missing_primitives.label: Integrate
candidate_missing_primitives.rationale: Combining multiple findings into a unified clinical picture is implied between Observe and Bound but has no explicit primitive

ambiguities.description: It is unclear whether plausibility evaluation and priority ordering are a single cognitive step or two; the blinded content treats them as separable but the allowed primitives do not clarify their relationship
Decide selects the next test or action; Act executes it. In some readings these collapse into one step, particularly when the decision is immediate and automatic

The blinded content specifies reassessment triggers when new findings conflict, but does not specify whether reassessment always re-enters at Observe or may short-circuit to Generate or Decide depending on conflict severity

notes: The artifact is a canonical iterative clinical reasoning loop. The process is fundamentally recursive: each verification either terminates the loop or re-seeds it. Evaluate is dominant because plausibility judgment governs both the candidate set and the branching logic throughout. No primitives from the allowed list appear spurious; Allocate, Coordinate, Schedule, Sequence, Synchronize, Communicate, Classify, Compare, and Reflect have no clear anchoring in the blinded procedural content as written, though Classify is a plausible near-miss for the implicit step of categorizing candidate explanations by type before Explain.
